projects.name: Glas Olsson
projects.shortDescription: A Laravel project for a fictional company with a functional admin panel to manage products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A Laravel project crafted by me and my teammate. The goal was to create a functional admin panel for a fictional company where the user can manage products and their categories. From scratch we created a full CRUD system for both products and categories.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: The website is built with Laravel and uses SQlite as the database, following the MVC model for secure routes. Tailwind CSS is used for styling to create a clean and responsive design. The Chart.js library is integrated to visualize sales data and provide insights into product performance.

descriptionSections.title: Process
descriptionSections.content: We started by setting up the Laravel project and designing the database schema for products and categories. We then implemented the CRUD functionality, ensuring that users can easily create, read, update, and delete products and categories. After that, we focused on styling the admin panel with Tailwind CSS to enhance the user experience. Finally, we integrated Chart.js to display sales data in an interactive and visually appealing way. Throughout the development process, we conducted rigorous testing and debugging to ensure that all features work seamlessly and that the user interface is intuitive and responsive.

descriptionSections.title: Results
descriptionSections.content: The admin panel successfully provides a user-friendly interface for managing products and categories, with all CRUD operations functioning as expected. Our rigerous testing and debugging process ensured a smooth user experience, and the integration of Chart.js allows for insightful data visualization of sales performance.

projects.name: Yrgopelago
projects.shortDescription: A fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Yrgopelago.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A fullstack project for a fictional hotel company called Yrgopelago.

descriptionSections.title: Core Functionality
descriptionSections.content: Complete hotel booking system where users can browse available rooms, make reservations, and manage their bookings.

descriptionSections.title: Additional Features
descriptionSections.content: Users can add premium services to their bookings such as breakfast packages and spa access.

descriptionSections.title: Tech Stack
descriptionSections.content: Built with JavaScript, PHP, and SQlite database with Composer for dependency management.

projects.name: Chirper
projects.shortDescription: A web application for interacting with a birdcall API.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: My first project working with external APIs and real-time data integration.

descriptionSections.title: Functionality
descriptionSections.content: A web application that makes API calls to a public data source and displays the information dynamically on the page.

descriptionSections.title: Tech & Learning
descriptionSections.content: Built with vanilla JavaScript and the Fetch API to master asynchronous data fetching and DOM manipulation.

descriptionSections.title: Status
descriptionSections.content: Completed in 2025 and now live on the web for public access.

projects.name: Butter-chicken
projects.shortDescription: A command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on budget.

descriptionSections.title: Overview
descriptionSections.content: A command-line application built with Node.js for finding optimal travel destinations based on budget.

descriptionSections.title: Purpose
descriptionSections.content: Helps users discover travel destinations that offer the best value for money within their specified budget constraints.

descriptionSections.title: Algorithm
descriptionSections.content: Uses mathematical calculations to analyze cost-to-experience ratios and rank destinations by value proposition.

descriptionSections.title: Details
descriptionSections.content: Completed in 2026 and available on GitHub for community contribution and feedback.
